title: Proseminar Humangeographie: Reclaiming the City: Women's Empowerment and Public Spaces in Middle Eastern Urban Landscapes
further_info: Recommended Reading Session 1: Women, public space, and the urban question in the Middle East Date: Friday, 24 April 2026, 14:00–18:00 • Massey, D. (1994). Space, place and gender. Cambridge: Polity Press. • • Lefebvre, H. (1996). “The right to the city.” In E. Kofman & E. Lebas (Eds.), Writings on cities (pp. 147–159). Oxford: Blackwell. • Alizadeh, H., Tuncer, S., Kohlbacher, J., & Karami, S. (2025). Women’s empowerment through public space: Gendered urban experiences across the Middle East and Europe. Cham: Springer. Session 2: Contestation, protest, and the politics of visibility Date: Friday, 22 May 2026, 14:00–18:00 • Kern, L. (2021). Feminist city: Claiming space in a man-made world. London: Verso. • Fenster, T. (2005). “The Right to the Gendered City: Different Formations of Belonging in Everyday Life.” Journal of Gender Studies, 14(3), 217–231. • Bayat, A. (2012). “Politics in the city-inside-out.” City & Society, 24(2), 110–128. Session 3: Urban governance, reform, and the future of inclusive public spaces Date: Friday, 19 June 2026, 14:00–18:00 • Crenshaw, K. (1991). “Mapping the margins: Intersectionality, identity politics, and violence against women of colour.” Stanford Law Review, 43(6), 1241–1299. • Arjmand, R. (2017). Public Urban Space, Gender and Segregation: Women and Public Spaces in Iran. London: Routledge. • Al Johani, A. (2025). Understanding the use and experience of quasi-public spaces from a gender perspective in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia (Doctoral dissertation, The University of Melbourne). Three online sessions and one in-person session at Heidelberg University are planned for the final student project presentations. - First session (online): Friday, 24 April 2026, 14:00–18:00 - Second session (online): Friday, 22 May 2026, 14:00–18:00 - Third session (online): Friday, 19 June 2026, 14:00–18:00 - Final session (in-person): Friday, 17 July 2026, 9:00–13:00.
